bead.title: pre-claim 'landing worktree has staged changes' against .ddx/beads.jsonl deadlocks workers
bead.description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim hook bails out with reason=preclaim_systemic when .ddx/beads.jsonl or .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl have staged changes after a 2-second wait. These two files are continuously updated by other workers as they record bead state and attempts metrics, so the 2s wait window is reliably too short on a multi-worker host. Result: pre-claim fails on every iteration, worker idles indefinitely, queue stops moving.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 04:30-04:48 EDT; ~18 minutes of continuous idle)
- Worker: PID 3296249, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 00:32 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779093...jsonl (binary v0.6.2-alpha103-51-g831debed2)
- Pattern every ~30s: resource.preflight -> loop.idle.
- loop.idle payload:
  {
    "detail": "landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s:\nM\t.ddx/beads.jsonl\nM\t.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
    "idle_interval": "30s",
    "reason": "preclaim_systemic"
  }
- The same detail string repeated 25+ times in 18 minutes. Worker successfully processed 1 bead earlier in its lifetime (claimed=1, results=1) but then got stuck on this pattern.
- A second fizeau-scoped daemon (PID 3795321) just started and is on its first resource.preflight; it will likely hit the same issue immediately.
- Two DDx beads are currently being executed by claude subprocesses (ddx-2af07fa2 etime 20m, ddx-fe2a5722 etime 4m) — those workers are continuously writing to .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl in their respective project worktrees, which propagates back through git operations to the fizeau landing worktree's view.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Tracker files are 'always dirty' in a multi-worker setup. .ddx/beads.jsonl changes every few seconds as workers heartbeat, claim, release, and update notes. .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl changes whenever a worker records an attempt. Waiting 2s for those to settle will reliably fail on a busy host.
2. The pre-claim hook's intent is to ensure no in-progress code work would be clobbered by claiming a new bead. Tracker files don't need that protection — they're not code, they're append-mostly metadata that the next claim will rewrite anyway.
3. The 30s loop.idle backoff multiplied by 25+ consecutive 'staged changes' detections means the worker is wasting >12 minutes of wall-clock time per claim cycle. Throughput collapses.
4. This is distinct from the related bugs ddx-f9ddaa68 (JSON decoder schema mismatch) and ddx-8f2e0ebf (wedge on route resolution). Same systemic-idle symptom, different root cause.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Tracker-file allowlist: pre-claim's 'is the landing worktree clean?' check must ignore .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, and any other DDx-managed metadata files. Only code/doc/test changes should block pre-claim.
B. Increase the timeout if a tracker-file-only wait is genuinely needed (rare): bump from 2s to 30s with structured logging on each retry so operators see what's blocking.
C. Distinguish 'transient' (tracker writes in flight) from 'systemic' (code changes never committed) in the reason field — currently both surface as preclaim_systemic which is misleading: this is transient contention, not a systemic configuration issue.
D. Operator-attention escalation: after N consecutive identical loop.idle.detail strings (default 5), emit an operator-attention event with the detail and bead-id, instead of silently looping.
E. Multi-worker coordination: if pre-claim sees specifically tracker files dirty AND another worker is in the middle of writing, defer with backoff rather than 'idle until clean'. The other worker WILL finish; pre-claim should wait for its commit, not for the working tree to magically clean itself.

CROSS-REF
Fourth bug in this session (cf. ddx-57c40485 index.lock; ddx-8f2e0ebf route-resolution wedge; ddx-f9ddaa68 pre_claim_intake decoder). All four share the theme: silent failure modes that look healthy via heartbeats but produce zero throughput. The user is currently unable to make progress on the claude-tui implementation chain because pre-claim won't let workers claim the next bead.
bead.acceptance:
1. Pre-claim's worktree-cleanliness check ignores .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, and other DDx-managed metadata files. Verified by a test that dirties those files and asserts pre-claim still succeeds.
2. Tracker-file-only dirty state is reported via a new reason code (e.g. preclaim_tracker_contention) distinct from preclaim_systemic. Tests cover both branches.
3. Same-detail escalation: after N (default 5) consecutive loop.idle events with identical detail, emit an operator-attention event with bead-id, detail, and elapsed-idle.
4. Documented contract in DDx README or AGENTS.md naming which files block pre-claim and which do not.
5. Regression test: a multi-worker integration test (3+ workers in same project) asserts that workers successfully claim beads in parallel even while .ddx/beads.jsonl receives concurrent writes.
